message:
test(conformance): extend groupby.rolling live-oracle to min/max/count

Extends the DataFrameGroupBy.rolling live-oracle conformance family
from the sum/mean pair landed in de68f8b to cover `.min()`,
`.max()`, and `.count()`. Same harness / oracle pattern; three new
FixtureOperation variants + three new live-oracle tests, all
reusing the existing `op_dataframe_groupby_rolling_builtin` and
`execute_dataframe_groupby_rolling_fixture_operation` dispatches.

crates/fp-conformance/oracle/pandas_oracle.py (+24):
- Three new thin wrappers:
  `op_dataframe_groupby_rolling_min`,
  `op_dataframe_groupby_rolling_max`,
  `op_dataframe_groupby_rolling_count`. Each forwards to
  `op_dataframe_groupby_rolling_builtin(pd, payload, <func>,
  <op_name>)` with func = "min" | "max" | "count" so the oracle
  shares the normalize_groupby_rolling_frame + groupby(...).
  rolling(window_size).<func>() pipeline with the sum/mean path.
- `dispatch` grows three new branches that accept both canonical
  and `data_frame_*` alias spellings, matching how
  dataframe_groupby_rolling_{mean,sum} are already wired.

crates/fp-conformance/src/lib.rs (+196):
- Three new FixtureOperation variants:
  `DataFrameGroupByRollingMin`, `DataFrameGroupByRollingMax`,
  `DataFrameGroupByRollingCount`, each with `#[serde(rename = ...,
  alias = "data_frame_*")]` matching the oracle spellings.
- `operation_name()` returns the canonical name string.
- All three are added to the CC-004 (rolling/resample family)
  contract row in `compat_contract_rows_for_operation`, the
  `expected_frame` dispatch arm in `fixture_expected` and
  `capture_live_oracle_expected`, and the dispatch arm in
  `execute_dataframe_fixture_operation` that routes to
  `execute_dataframe_groupby_rolling_fixture_operation`.
- `execute_dataframe_groupby_rolling_fixture_operation` grows
  `DataFrameGroupByRollingMin`/`Max`/`Count` arms that call
  `groupby.rolling(window_size).min()`, `.max()`, `.count()`
  respectively, with `err.to_string()` propagation.
- `execute_and_compare_differential` routes the three new variants
  through the shared live-oracle dispatch.
- Three new `#[test]`s mirroring the mean/sum tests:
    - `live_oracle_dataframe_groupby_rolling_min_matches_pandas`
      (FP-P2D-446)
    - `live_oracle_dataframe_groupby_rolling_max_matches_pandas`
      (FP-P2D-447)
    - `live_oracle_dataframe_groupby_rolling_count_matches_pandas`
      (FP-P2D-448)
  Each uses the same interleaved 8-row fixture shape (grp=a/b,
  float64 `val` column with a deliberate null) and `window_size=2`
  so behavior is directly comparable to the sum/mean fixtures.
  Each calls `assert_live_oracle_dataframe_groupby_rolling_frame_parity`
  which skips cleanly on missing pandas.

No fp-frame production change; the fp-frame
`groupby.rolling(n).{min,max,count}()` path was already wired --
this commit just freezes it against live pandas.

files.patch:

     )

+        pd, payload, "min", "dataframe_groupby_rolling_min"
+    )

+def op_dataframe_groupby_rolling_max(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    return op_dataframe_groupby_rolling_builtin(
+        pd, payload, "max", "dataframe_groupby_rolling_max"
+    )
+
+
+def op_dataframe_groupby_rolling_count(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    return op_dataframe_groupby_rolling_builtin(
+        pd, payload, "count", "dataframe_groupby_rolling_count"
+    )
+
+

     frame_payload = payload.get("frame")
     groupby_columns = payload.get("groupby_columns")
@@ -4598,6 +4616,12 @@ def dispatch(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:

     if op in {"dataframe_groupby_rolling_sum", "data_frame_groupby_rolling_sum"}:
         return op_dataframe_groupby_rolling_sum(pd, payload)
+    if op in {"dataframe_groupby_rolling_min", "data_frame_groupby_rolling_min"}:
+        return op_dataframe_groupby_rolling_min(pd, payload)
+    if op in {"dataframe_groupby_rolling_max", "data_frame_groupby_rolling_max"}:
+        return op_dataframe_groupby_rolling_max(pd, payload)
+    if op in {"dataframe_groupby_rolling_count", "data_frame_groupby_rolling_count"}:
+        return op_dataframe_groupby_rolling_count(pd, payload)
     if op in {"dataframe_groupby_cumcount", "data_frame_groupby_cumcount"}:
         return op_dataframe_groupby_cumcount(pd, payload)
     if op in {"dataframe_groupby_ngroup", "data_frame_groupby_ngroup"}:

@@ -27429,6 +27477,150 @@ mod tests {

     }
 
+    #[test]
+    fn live_oracle_dataframe_groupby_rolling_min_matches_pandas() {
+        let fixture: super::PacketFixture = serde_json::from_value(serde_json::json!({
+            "packet_id": "FP-P2D-446",
+            "case_id": "dataframe_groupby_rolling_min_live",
+            "mode": "strict",
+            "operation": "dataframe_groupby_rolling_min",
+            "oracle_source": "live_legacy_pandas",
+            "groupby_columns": ["grp"],
+            "window_size": 2,
+            "frame": {
+                "index": [
+                    { "kind": "int64", "value": 0 },
+                    { "kind": "int64", "value": 1 },
+                    { "kind": "int64", "value": 2 },
+                    { "kind": "int64", "value": 3 },
+                    { "kind": "int64", "value": 4 },
+                    { "kind": "int64", "value": 5 },
+                    { "kind": "int64", "value": 6 }
+                ],
+                "column_order": ["grp", "val"],
+                "columns": {
+                    "grp": [
+                        { "kind": "utf8", "value": "a" },
+                        { "kind": "utf8", "value": "b" },
+                        { "kind": "utf8", "value": "a" },
+                        { "kind": "utf8", "value": "b" },
+                        { "kind": "utf8", "value": "a" },
+                        { "kind": "utf8", "value": "b" },
+                        { "kind": "utf8", "value": "a" }
+                    ],
+                    "val": [
+                        { "kind": "float64", "value": 5.0 },
+                        { "kind": "float64", "value": 2.0 },
+                        { "kind": "float64", "value": 1.0 },
+                        { "kind": "float64", "value": 7.0 },
+                        { "kind": "null", "value": "na_n" },
+                        { "kind": "float64", "value": 6.0 },
+                        { "kind": "float64", "value": 4.0 }
+                    ]
+                }
+            }
+        }))
+        .expect("fixture");
+
+        assert_live_oracle_dataframe_groupby_rolling_frame_parity(fixture);
+    }
+

     #[test]
     fn live_oracle_dataframe_groupby_resample_min_matches_pandas() {
         let fixture: super::PacketFixture = serde_json::from_value(serde_json::json!({
